title: CSV Row Count
prompt: Read the file data.csv and count the number of data rows (excluding the header). Write a Python script solution.py that outputs the count to result.txt.

input.data.csv:
name,age,city
Alice,30,Portland
Bob,25,Seattle
Carla,28,Denver
Dave,35,Austin
Eve,22,Boston

title: CSV Column Sum
prompt: Read data.csv and compute the sum of the 'amount' column. Write solution.py that saves the sum (as a plain number) to total.txt.

input.data.csv:
item,amount
Apples,12
Bananas,7
Cherries,23
Dates,5
Elderberries,8

title: Filter CSV by Value
prompt: Read data.csv and filter rows where the 'status' column equals 'active'. Write solution.py that outputs the filtered data (with header) to filtered.csv.

title: JSON to CSV Conversion
prompt: Read data.json which contains an array of objects with keys 'name', 'score', and 'grade'. Write solution.py that converts it to output.csv with a header row and the same columns.

title: CSV to JSON Conversion
prompt: Read data.csv and convert it to a JSON array of objects. Write solution.py that outputs the result to output.json. The JSON must be valid and contain one object per row.

title: Sort CSV by Column
prompt: Read data.csv and sort the rows by the 'price' column in ascending numeric order. Write solution.py that outputs the sorted CSV (with header) to sorted.csv.

input.data.csv:
product,price
Widget,15.99
Gadget,9.49
Doohickey,22.00
Thingamajig,3.75
Whatsit,11.20

title: Remove Duplicate Rows
prompt: Read data.csv which contains duplicate rows. Write solution.py that removes exact duplicate rows (keeping the first occurrence) and writes the deduplicated data with header to clean.csv.

input.data.csv:
email,name
alice@test.com,Alice
bob@test.com,Bob
alice@test.com,Alice
carla@test.com,Carla
bob@test.com,Bob
eve@test.com,Eve

title: Compute Column Average
prompt: Read data.csv and compute the average of the 'temperature' column. Write solution.py that writes the average rounded to 1 decimal place to avg.txt.

input.data.csv:
day,temperature
Mon,22.5
Tue,24.1
Wed,19.8
Thu,21.3
Fri,26.0
Sat,23.7
Sun,20.6

title: Extract Unique Values
prompt: Read data.csv and extract all unique values from the 'category' column. Write solution.py that outputs them sorted alphabetically, one per line, to categories.txt.

title: JSON Key Extraction
prompt: Read data.json and extract only the 'name' and 'email' fields from each object. Write solution.py that outputs a new JSON array containing objects with only those two keys to output.json.

title: Count Values by Group
prompt: Read data.csv and count how many rows exist for each unique value in the 'department' column. Write solution.py that outputs a CSV with columns 'department' and 'count', sorted by department name, to summary.csv.

title: Add Computed Column
prompt: Read data.csv with columns 'item', 'quantity', and 'unit_price'. Write solution.py that adds a 'total' column (quantity * unit_price) and writes the result to output.csv.

title: Find Maximum Value Row
prompt: Read data.csv and find the row with the highest 'score' value. Write solution.py that outputs a single line in the format 'name: score' to winner.txt.

input.data.csv:
name,score
Alpha,87
Beta,92
Gamma,78
Delta,95
Epsilon,88

title: Trim Whitespace in CSV
prompt: Read data.csv where some fields have leading/trailing whitespace. Write solution.py that trims all whitespace from every field and writes the cleaned data to clean.csv.

input.data.csv:
name, city , score
 Alice , Portland ,90
  Bob,Seattle ,85
Carla,  Denver, 78
 Dave , Austin ,92

title: Word Frequency Count
prompt: Read text.txt and count the frequency of each word (case-insensitive). Write solution.py that outputs a JSON object mapping each lowercase word to its count in freq.json. Ignore punctuation.

input.text.txt: The cat sat on the mat. The cat was happy. A happy cat is a good cat.

title: Min and Max from Column
prompt: Read data.csv and find the minimum and maximum values in the 'value' column. Write solution.py that writes two lines to stats.txt: 'min: X' and 'max: Y'.

input.data.csv:
label,value
A,45
B,12
C,67
D,34
E,89
F,23

title: Select CSV Columns
prompt: Read data.csv which has columns id, first_name, last_name, email, phone. Write solution.py that outputs only the columns first_name, last_name, and email (in that order) to subset.csv with a header row.

title: Reverse CSV Row Order
prompt: Read data.csv and reverse the order of the data rows (keep the header at the top). Write solution.py that outputs the reversed CSV to reversed.csv.

title: Count Non-Empty Fields
prompt: Read data.csv which has some empty fields. Write solution.py that counts how many non-empty values exist in the 'notes' column and writes the count to count.txt.

title: Validate JSON Structure
prompt: Read data.json and check that every object in the array has the required keys: 'id', 'name', and 'email'. Write solution.py that writes 'VALID' to result.txt if all objects pass, or 'INVALID: missing <key> in record <index>' for the first failing record (0-indexed).

title: Concatenate CSV Files
prompt: Read part1.csv and part2.csv which have the same columns. Write solution.py that concatenates them into a single combined.csv with one header row.

title: Key-Value File to JSON
prompt: Read config.txt which contains key=value pairs, one per line. Write solution.py that converts it to a JSON object in config.json.

title: CSV String Case Normalization
prompt: Read data.csv and normalize the 'name' column to title case (first letter of each word capitalized). Write solution.py that outputs the result to normalized.csv.

title: Flatten JSON One Level
prompt: Read data.json containing an object with nested objects one level deep. Write solution.py that flattens it so nested keys become 'parent_child' (underscore-separated). Output to flat.json.

title: Top N Rows from CSV
prompt: Read data.csv and extract the top 3 rows by 'revenue' (descending). Write solution.py that outputs these rows with header to top3.csv.

input.data.csv:
company,revenue
Acme,50000
Globex,120000
Initech,35000
Umbrella,95000
Wayne,200000
Stark,150000

title: CSV Numeric Range Filter
prompt: Read data.csv and filter rows where 'age' is between 25 and 35 (inclusive). Write solution.py that outputs matching rows with header to filtered.csv.

input.data.csv:
name,age,dept
Alice,30,HR
Bob,22,Eng
Carla,35,Sales
Dave,40,HR
Eve,25,Eng
Frank,19,Sales

title: Rename CSV Columns
prompt: Read data.csv and rename columns: 'fname' -> 'first_name', 'lname' -> 'last_name', 'dob' -> 'date_of_birth'. Write solution.py that outputs the renamed CSV to output.csv.

title: Split CSV into Files by Category
prompt: Read data.csv and split it into separate CSV files based on the 'type' column. Each output file should be named '<type>.csv' (lowercase) and contain only rows of that type plus a header. Write solution.py.

title: CSV Inner Join
prompt: Read users.csv and orders.csv. Perform an inner join on 'user_id'. Write solution.py that outputs joined.csv with columns: user_id, name, order_id, amount.

title: Parse Apache Log Status Codes
prompt: Read access.log (Apache Combined Log Format). Write solution.py that counts occurrences of each HTTP status code and outputs a JSON object mapping status code strings to counts in status_counts.json, sorted by count descending.

title: CSV Group-By Aggregation
prompt: Read sales.csv with columns 'region', 'product', and 'revenue'. Write solution.py that groups by 'region', computes total revenue per region, and outputs region_totals.csv with columns 'region' and 'total_revenue', sorted by total_revenue descending.

input.sales.csv:
region,product,revenue
North,Widget,1500
South,Gadget,2300
North,Gadget,1800
East,Widget,900
South,Widget,2100
North,Doohickey,750
East,Gadget,1200
South,Doohickey,1600

title: Nested JSON Flattening to CSV
prompt: Read data.json which contains an array of objects with nested 'address' sub-objects. Write solution.py that flattens each record so address fields become 'address_street', 'address_city', 'address_zip' and writes the flat data to output.csv.

title: Date Format Standardization
prompt: Read data.csv where the 'date' column has mixed formats: MM/DD/YYYY, DD-MM-YYYY, and YYYY.MM.DD. Write solution.py that standardizes all dates to ISO format YYYY-MM-DD and outputs the cleaned data to clean.csv.

title: Median and Standard Deviation
prompt: Read data.csv and compute the median and standard deviation (population) of the 'value' column. Write solution.py that outputs a JSON object with keys 'median' and 'std_dev' (both rounded to 2 decimal places) to stats.json.

title: Extract IPs from Log File
prompt: Read access.log and extract all unique IP addresses. Write solution.py that outputs them sorted alphabetically, one per line, to ips.txt. Also write the total count as the first line (e.g., 'Total: 5').

title: Running Total Column
prompt: Read data.csv with columns 'date' and 'amount'. Write solution.py that adds a 'running_total' column showing the cumulative sum of 'amount' and outputs to output.csv.

title: JSON Array Filtering and Sorting
prompt: Read data.json containing an array of product objects with 'name', 'price', and 'in_stock' (boolean). Write solution.py that filters to only in-stock items, sorts by price ascending, and writes the result to filtered.json.

title: Handle Missing CSV Values
prompt: Read data.csv which has missing values (empty fields). Write solution.py that replaces missing 'score' values with the column's mean (rounded to nearest integer) and missing 'grade' values with 'N/A'. Output to filled.csv.

input.data.csv:
name,score,grade
Alice,85,B
Bob,,A
Carla,90,
Dave,70,C
Eve,,
Frank,95,A

title: Frequency Distribution Histogram
prompt: Read data.csv with a 'score' column (integer values 0-100). Write solution.py that creates a frequency distribution with bins [0-20), [20-40), [40-60), [60-80), [80-100]. Output a CSV with columns 'bin' and 'count' to histogram.csv.

title: INI File to JSON Conversion
prompt: Read config.ini and convert it to a JSON object where sections become top-level keys and their key-value pairs become nested objects. Write solution.py that outputs to config.json.

input.config.ini:
[database]
host=localhost
port=5432
name=mydb

[server]
host=0.0.0.0
port=8080
workers=4

[logging]
level=info
file=app.log

title: CSV Left Join
prompt: Read employees.csv and departments.csv. Perform a left join on 'dept_id', keeping all employees. If an employee's department is not found, set dept_name to 'Unknown'. Write solution.py that outputs to joined.csv with columns: emp_id, name, dept_id, dept_name.

title: Time Interval Calculation
prompt: Read events.csv with columns 'event' and 'timestamp' (ISO format). Write solution.py that computes the time interval in seconds between consecutive events and outputs a CSV with columns 'from_event', 'to_event', 'seconds' to intervals.csv.

title: Log Error Pattern Extraction
prompt: Read app.log and extract all lines containing 'ERROR'. For each error, capture the timestamp and message. Write solution.py that outputs a JSON array of objects with 'timestamp' and 'message' keys to errors.json.

input.app.log:
2025-03-01 10:00:01 INFO  Server started on port 8080
2025-03-01 10:05:23 ERROR Database connection timeout
2025-03-01 10:05:30 INFO  Retrying connection...
2025-03-01 10:05:45 ERROR Retry failed: max attempts exceeded
2025-03-01 10:06:00 WARN  Falling back to cache
2025-03-01 10:10:00 INFO  Cache hit rate: 85%
2025-03-01 10:15:00 ERROR Disk space below threshold: 2GB remaining
2025-03-01 10:20:00 INFO  Cleanup completed

title: CSV Pivot Table
prompt: Read sales.csv with columns 'quarter', 'product', and 'units'. Write solution.py that creates a pivot table where rows are products, columns are quarters, and values are units. Output to pivot.csv. Use 0 for missing combinations.

input.sales.csv:
quarter,product,units
Q1,Widget,100
Q1,Gadget,150
Q2,Widget,120
Q2,Gadget,130
Q2,Doohickey,80
Q3,Widget,90
Q3,Doohickey,110
Q4,Widget,140
Q4,Gadget,160
Q4,Doohickey,95

title: Detect Outliers with IQR
prompt: Read data.csv and detect outliers in the 'value' column using the IQR method (values below Q1-1.5*IQR or above Q3+1.5*IQR). Write solution.py that outputs outlier rows (with header) to outliers.csv and the count to stdout.

title: Merge JSON Objects
prompt: Read base.json and override.json. Write solution.py that deep-merges them (override takes precedence for conflicting keys, nested objects are merged recursively). Output to merged.json.

title: Cross-Field Validation
prompt: Read data.csv with columns 'start_date', 'end_date', and 'status'. Write solution.py that validates: (1) end_date >= start_date, (2) if status is 'completed', end_date must not be empty. Write rows failing validation to errors.csv with an extra 'error' column describing the issue.

title: Moving Average Calculation
prompt: Read data.csv with columns 'date' and 'price'. Write solution.py that computes a 3-day simple moving average of 'price'. Output a CSV with columns 'date', 'price', 'sma_3' to output.csv. For the first 2 rows where SMA cannot be computed, use empty string for sma_3.

title: JSON Schema Validator
prompt: Write solution.py that reads data.json (array of user objects) and validates each against this schema: 'name' (string, required), 'age' (integer, 0-150, required), 'email' (string containing '@', required). Output valid records to valid.json and invalid records (with 'errors' key added) to invalid.json.

title: N-gram Text Analysis
prompt: Read text.txt and compute all bigrams (2-word sequences) with their frequencies. Write solution.py that outputs a JSON object mapping bigrams (words joined by space) to counts in bigrams.json, including only bigrams appearing 2+ times.

input.text.txt: the quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog the quick brown dog jumps over the lazy fox the quick red fox runs over the hill

title: CSV Column Type Detection
prompt: Read data.csv and detect the data type of each column (integer, float, date, or string). Write solution.py that outputs a JSON object mapping column names to detected types in types.json.

title: Log Response Time Percentiles
prompt: Read access.log where each line ends with a response time in ms. Write solution.py that computes the 50th, 90th, 95th, and 99th percentile response times and outputs them as a JSON object with keys 'p50', 'p90', 'p95', 'p99' to percentiles.json.

input.access.log:
GET /api/users 200 45ms
GET /api/posts 200 120ms
POST /api/login 200 89ms
GET /api/users 200 52ms
GET /api/posts 200 230ms
GET /api/data 200 67ms
POST /api/submit 200 340ms
GET /api/health 200 12ms
GET /api/users 200 48ms
GET /api/posts 500 890ms
GET /api/data 200 55ms
POST /api/login 200 95ms
GET /api/health 200 15ms
GET /api/users 200 42ms
GET /api/posts 200 110ms
GET /api/data 200 78ms
POST /api/submit 200 280ms
GET /api/health 200 10ms
GET /api/users 200 51ms
GET /api/posts 200 145ms

title: Transpose CSV
prompt: Read data.csv and transpose it so that rows become columns and columns become rows. The first column of the input becomes the header of the output. Write solution.py that saves the transposed data to transposed.csv.

title: Correlate Two Columns
prompt: Read data.csv with columns 'hours_studied' and 'exam_score'. Write solution.py that computes the Pearson correlation coefficient between them (rounded to 4 decimal places) and writes it to correlation.txt.

input.data.csv:
student,hours_studied,exam_score
A,2,55
B,4,65
C,6,72
D,8,85
E,3,60
F,7,80
G,5,68
H,9,90
I,1,45
J,10,95

title: Multi-File JSON Merge
prompt: Read users_1.json and users_2.json, each containing arrays of user objects with 'id' and 'name'. Some users appear in both files with different data. Write solution.py that merges them, deduplicating by 'id' (users_2 values take priority). Output the merged, sorted-by-id array to merged.json.

title: CSV Percentage Calculation
prompt: Read data.csv with columns 'category' and 'count'. Write solution.py that adds a 'percentage' column showing each category's share of the total (rounded to 1 decimal place, e.g. '34.5'). Output to output.csv. The percentages should sum to approximately 100.

input.data.csv:
category,count
Electronics,450
Clothing,320
Food,180
Books,150
Toys,100

title: Multi-Key CSV Pivot with Aggregation
prompt: Read sales.csv with columns 'year', 'quarter', 'region', 'product', and 'revenue'. Write solution.py that creates a pivot table: rows are (region, product) pairs, columns are 'year_quarter' combinations (e.g., '2024_Q1'), values are summed revenue. Fill missing cells with 0. Sort rows by region then product. Output to pivot.csv.

title: Log Session Reconstruction
prompt: Read access.log with timestamped requests including session IDs. Write solution.py that reconstructs user sessions: group requests by session_id, compute session duration (first to last request), count pages visited, and identify the most visited page per session. Output a JSON array of session objects with keys 'session_id', 'duration_seconds', 'page_count', 'top_page' to sessions.json, sorted by duration descending.

input.access.log:
2025-01-15T10:00:00 sess_A GET /home
2025-01-15T10:00:30 sess_B GET /home
2025-01-15T10:01:00 sess_A GET /products
2025-01-15T10:01:30 sess_B GET /about
2025-01-15T10:02:00 sess_A GET /products
2025-01-15T10:02:30 sess_C GET /home
2025-01-15T10:03:00 sess_A GET /cart
2025-01-15T10:03:30 sess_B GET /home
2025-01-15T10:04:00 sess_C GET /products
2025-01-15T10:05:00 sess_A GET /checkout
2025-01-15T10:05:30 sess_C GET /home
2025-01-15T10:06:00 sess_B GET /contact

title: Data Pipeline: Clean, Transform, Aggregate
prompt: Read raw_sales.csv which has messy data: inconsistent date formats, missing values, duplicate rows, and whitespace issues. Write solution.py that: (1) standardizes dates to YYYY-MM-DD, (2) removes exact duplicate rows, (3) fills missing 'quantity' with 1 and missing 'unit_price' with the product's average unit_price, (4) adds a 'total' column (quantity * unit_price), (5) groups by product and outputs summary.csv with columns 'product', 'total_quantity', 'total_revenue', 'avg_price' (rounded to 2 decimals), sorted by total_revenue descending.

title: JSON Tree to Flat Table
prompt: Read org.json which contains a recursive tree structure representing an org chart (each node has 'name', 'title', and optional 'reports' array of child nodes). Write solution.py that flattens this into a CSV with columns 'name', 'title', 'manager', 'depth' (root is depth 0, manager is empty for root). Output to org_flat.csv, sorted by depth then name.

title: Time Series Peak Detection
prompt: Read data.csv with columns 'timestamp' and 'value'. Write solution.py that detects local peaks (a value strictly greater than both its neighbors). Output a JSON array of objects with 'timestamp', 'value', and 'peak_index' (0-based row index) to peaks.json. Also compute and print the average time between consecutive peaks in seconds to stdout.

title: TF-IDF Word Scoring
prompt: Read documents.json containing an array of document strings. Write solution.py that computes TF-IDF scores for each word in each document. Output a JSON object where keys are document indices (as strings '0','1',...) and values are objects mapping words to their TF-IDF scores (rounded to 4 decimal places). Write to tfidf.json. Use log base-e for IDF. Ignore punctuation and convert to lowercase.

input.documents.json: ["the cat sat on the mat","the dog sat on the log","the cat chased the dog","a mat and a log"]

title: Multi-Format Log Analyzer
prompt: Read mixed.log which contains lines in three formats: (1) Apache-style: 'IP - - [timestamp] "METHOD /path HTTP/1.1" STATUS SIZE', (2) JSON-style: '{"level":"...","msg":"...","ts":"..."}', (3) Syslog-style: 'MMM DD HH:MM:SS hostname process: message'. Write solution.py that parses all three formats and outputs a unified JSON array sorted by timestamp, each entry having keys 'timestamp', 'type' (apache/json/syslog), 'message'. Output to unified.json.

title: Data Normalization Pipeline
prompt: Read data.csv with numeric columns 'feature_a', 'feature_b', 'feature_c'. Write solution.py that: (1) computes min-max normalization for each column to [0,1] range, (2) computes z-score normalization for each column, (3) outputs minmax_normalized.csv and zscore_normalized.csv with the same column structure. Round all values to 4 decimal places.

title: Relational Data Integrity Check
prompt: Read three files: users.csv (id, name), orders.csv (order_id, user_id, product_id, amount), products.csv (product_id, name, price). Write solution.py that checks referential integrity: (1) every user_id in orders exists in users, (2) every product_id in orders exists in products, (3) no duplicate primary keys. Output a JSON report to integrity.json with keys 'orphan_user_ids', 'orphan_product_ids', 'duplicate_users', 'duplicate_products', 'duplicate_orders' (each an array), and 'is_valid' (boolean).

title: Sentiment Word Scoring
prompt: Read reviews.json (array of review strings) and lexicon.json (object mapping words to sentiment scores from -5 to 5). Write solution.py that scores each review by summing the sentiment scores of its words (lowercase, ignoring unknown words and punctuation). Output a JSON array of objects with 'review_index', 'score', 'label' (positive if score>0, negative if score<0, neutral if 0) to sentiment.json, plus a summary object in summary.json with 'avg_score' (rounded to 2 decimals), 'positive_count', 'negative_count', 'neutral_count'.

input.reviews.json: ["The food was great and the service was excellent","Terrible experience awful food and rude staff","It was okay nothing special","Amazing place wonderful food highly recommend","Bad service but decent food"]

title: CSV Diff Report
prompt: Read old.csv and new.csv which have the same columns with 'id' as primary key. Write solution.py that produces a diff report in diff.json: (1) 'added' - rows in new but not old, (2) 'removed' - rows in old but not new, (3) 'modified' - rows present in both but with changed values (include old and new values for changed fields). Each category is an array of objects.

title: Weighted Scoring with Rank Assignment
prompt: Read scores.csv with columns 'name', 'test1', 'test2', 'test3', 'homework', 'participation'. Read weights.json mapping each column to its weight (0-1, summing to 1). Write solution.py that computes weighted scores, assigns ranks (1=highest, handle ties with same rank), and determines letter grades (A>=90, B>=80, C>=70, D>=60, F<60). Output results.csv with columns 'rank', 'name', 'weighted_score' (2 decimals), 'grade', sorted by rank ascending.

title: Event Log State Machine
prompt: Read events.csv with columns 'entity_id', 'timestamp', 'event_type'. Valid state transitions are: created->active, active->paused, active->completed, paused->active, paused->cancelled. Write solution.py that: (1) reconstructs final state for each entity, (2) identifies invalid transitions, (3) outputs entity_states.json (mapping entity_id to final state) and violations.json (array of objects with 'entity_id', 'from_state', 'to_state', 'timestamp').

title: Data Quality Report Generator
prompt: Read data.csv and generate a comprehensive data quality report. Write solution.py that analyzes every column and outputs report.json containing: (1) 'row_count' and 'column_count', (2) per-column stats in a 'columns' object keyed by column name, each with 'dtype' (integer/float/string/date), 'null_count', 'null_pct' (rounded to 1 decimal), 'unique_count', 'sample_values' (first 3 non-null values). For numeric columns, also include 'min', 'max', 'mean' (2 decimals), 'median'. For string columns, include 'min_length', 'max_length', 'avg_length' (1 decimal). Also include 'duplicate_rows' count and 'completeness_score' (pct of non-null cells, 1 decimal).
